channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped rubbish and blocked pavement for a few days, 30th June
item.description:
Why do you let people working at shop mess streets with rubbish and Blocking pavement ?

item.title: Trees, bushes, stinging nettles overgrown, 30th June
item.description:
The trees, bushes, stinging nettles etc are all overgrown along the roadside as you approach/drive past cornerstone and shenstone schools . This makes it difficult for drivers it is restricts view. Also parking on the side of the road at school pickup, which is often necessary due to volume of cars, becomes impossible
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Halt Robin Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge fly tipping, 30th June
item.description:
Flytipping

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chester Gardens, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped brown bin, 30th June
item.description:
Dumped brown bin VERY HEAVY with all-sorts of rubbish please make your crew aware this is very heavy might need 3 men to clear .

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tyrrell Avenue, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous parking, 30th June
item.description:
Every day we are getting vehicles illegally parked on the bend in Clayhill crescent wh is very dangerous. There was a van but now there is a a car fully parked and it’s clearly marked no parking
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Clayhill Crescent, Chinbrook, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Forest bikes blocking pavement, 30th June
item.description:
These have been dumped then fallen over and mean that elderly or disabled people, or anyone with a pram or pushchair, cannot get past.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cline Road, Muswell Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road closure, 30th June
item.description:
Outside 2 summer hill villas, Susanwood there are tradesman working on the roof they have closed the road without given notice to the residence of the road and using it as a work area. They have just put cones out without given any additional warning or signage out.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Susan Wood, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Dead tree

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Polygon Road, Somers Town, Greater London

item.title: Rubbish constantly dumped around Dulwich Plough bus stop, 30th June
item.description:
There's an endless stream of rubbish being dumped around the Dulwich Plough bus stop. Foxes get into the bags overnight and every single morning there is litter everywhere. Mostly it's coming from the flats above the row of shops but there's also commercial waste too.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 7 days ago a dust cart spilled oil/fluid outside of 211, 209, 207 Maidstone Road., 30th June
item.description:
The dustcart had a spillage, oil/fluid covered the road. After a while bags of green stuff was sprinkled onto the road to soak it up. This then turned brown. We were informed that this was dangerous and would be cleaned up the following day. Two men turned up a couple of days later , spent some time looking at it but nothing has been done. The orange waste is over the road with bits of plastic bags ( which it was contained in) and is now being blown onto the grass verge. As we were advised by the refuse collecting this was dangerous, I am concerned that when it rains, this orange dust is going to come onto my front garden and into the neighbouring field where we have horses. We also have dogs. People are walking through it and cars driving over it. Is it possible for it to be cleaned up. Also worried about the wildlife and consequently any contamination this may cause. Especially as we were told it was a danger.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 169, Maidstone Road, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
